NCT00424476.name: BLISS-52
NCT00424476.group: Seropositive SLE + SOC, belimumab 10 mg/kg IV Q4W vs placebo (wk 52, SRI-4 RR)

allOutcomes.title: SLE Responder Index-4 at week 52 (SRI-4 composite primary) (RR)

NCT00424476.snippet: Source: Navarra SV et al. Lancet 2011;377:721-731 (BLISS-52).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 867 adults with active seropositive SLE (SELENA-SLEDAI >=6, autoantibody-positive) on stable standard of care randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to belimumab 1 mg/kg IV (n=288), belimumab 10 mg/kg IV (n=290), or matched placebo (n=287) every 4 weeks for 52 weeks. Primary comparison for this app: 10 mg/kg vs placebo (n=205 vs 213 after per-protocol adjustment per NEJM figure).

evidence.label: Primary - SRI-4 at Week 52
evidence.text: SLE Responder Index-4 response rate at week 52 (composite: >=4-point reduction in SELENA-SLEDAI, no worsening on BILAG or PGA): belimumab 10 mg/kg 57.6% (118/205) vs placebo 43.6% (93/213); risk ratio 1.32 (95% CI 1.11-1.57; P=0.0006). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Steroid Reduction, Flares, Safety
evidence.text: Steroid reduction (>=25% from >=7.5 mg/d): favoured belimumab. Severe flare rate reduced. Infusion reactions 17% vs 15%. Depression/suicidality signals emerged with larger pooled analyses post-hoc (not in this trial alone).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind. D3 LOW - 93% completion. D4 LOW - central SELENA-SLEDAI training. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Clinical diagnosis of SLE by ACR criteria.~* Active SLE disease.~* Autoantibody-positive.~* On stable SLE treatment regimen.~Key Exclusion Criteria:~* Pregnant or nursing~* Have received treatment with any B cell targeted therapy.~* Have received treatment with a biological investigational agent in the past year.~* Have received IV cyclophosphamide within 180 days of Day 0.~* Have severe lupus kidney disease.~* Have active central nervous system (CNS) lupus.~* Have req...

NCT00410384.name: BLISS-76
NCT00410384.group: Seropositive SLE + SOC, belimumab 10 mg/kg IV Q4W vs placebo (wk 52, SRI-4 RR)

allOutcomes.title: SLE Responder Index-4 at week 52 (SRI-4 composite primary) (RR)

NCT00410384.snippet: Source: Furie R et al. Arthritis Rheum 2011;63:3918-3930 (BLISS-76).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 819 adults with active seropositive SLE on stable SOC randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to belimumab 1 mg/kg IV, 10 mg/kg IV, or placebo every 4 weeks for 72 weeks. Primary SRI-4 endpoint at week 52. Primary comparison for this app: 10 mg/kg vs placebo (n=197 vs 195).

evidence.label: Primary - SRI-4 at Week 52
evidence.text: SRI-4 response at week 52: belimumab 10 mg/kg 43.2% (85/197) vs placebo 33.8% (66/195); risk ratio 1.28 (95% CI 1.02-1.60; P=0.02). Primary endpoint met. Effect attenuated by week 76.

evidence.label: Secondary - Steroid Tapering, Flares, Safety
evidence.text: Steroid-tapering not significant in BLISS-76. Severe-flare rate numerically lower. Mortality balanced. Infections and infusion reactions similar between arms.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind. D3 LOW - 88% completion. D4 LOW - central SLEDAI. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Clinical diagnosis of SLE by ACR criteria.~* Active SLE disease.~* Autoantibody-positive.~* On stable SLE treatment regimen.~Key Exclusion Criteria:~* Pregnant or nursing~* Have received treatment with any B cell targeted therapy.~* Have received treatment with a biological investigational agent in the past year.~* Have received IV cyclophosphamide within 180 days of Day 0.~* Have severe lupus kidney disease.~* Have active central nervous system (CNS) lupus.~* Have req...

NCT01484496.name: BLISS-SC
NCT01484496.group: Seropositive SLE + SOC, belimumab 200 mg SC weekly vs placebo (wk 52, SRI-4 RR)

allOutcomes.title: SLE Responder Index-4 at week 52 (SRI-4 composite primary) (RR)

NCT01484496.snippet: Source: Stohl W et al. Arthritis Rheumatol 2017;69:1016-1027 (BLISS-SC).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 839 adults with active seropositive SLE on stable SOC randomized 2:1 double-blind to belimumab 200 mg subcutaneous weekly (n=556) or matched placebo SC weekly (n=280) for 52 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary - SRI-4 at Week 52
evidence.text: SRI-4 response at week 52: belimumab SC 61.4% (341/556) vs placebo 48.4% (136/280); risk ratio 1.27 (95% CI 1.11-1.44); odds ratio 1.68 (95% CI 1.25-2.25; P=0.0006). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Flare Reduction and Steroid Tapering
evidence.text: Time to first severe flare prolonged (HR 0.51). Sustained steroid reduction >=25% from baseline: 18.2% belimumab vs 11.9% placebo (P=0.03). Injection-site reactions 6% vs 2%. No new safety signals.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind matched SC placebo. D3 LOW - 85% completion. D4 LOW - central SLEDAI. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. At least 18 years of age.~2. On stable SLE treatment regimen which may include corticosteroids (for example, prednisone), antimalarial (for example, hydroxychloroquine) and/or immunosuppressants (for example, azathioprine, methotrexate, mycophenolate, etc.)~Exclusion Criteria:~1. NCT01639339.name: BLISS-LN
NCT01639339.group: Active lupus nephritis + SOC (MMF or CYC), belimumab 10 mg/kg IV vs placebo (wk 104, PERR RR)

allOutcomes.title: Primary Efficacy Renal Response (PERR) at week 104 (primary) (RR)

NCT01639339.snippet: Source: Furie R et al. NEJM 2020;383:1117-1128 (BLISS-LN).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 446 adults with biopsy-confirmed active class III/IV/V lupus nephritis randomized 1:1 double-blind to belimumab 10 mg/kg IV monthly (n=223) or placebo (n=223) plus standard induction (cyclophosphamide or mycophenolate) then mycophenolate maintenance, over 104 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary - PERR at Week 104
evidence.text: Primary Efficacy Renal Response at 104 weeks (UPCR <=0.7, eGFR not >20% below preflare baseline, no rescue therapy): belimumab 43% (96/223) vs placebo 32% (72/223); risk ratio 1.34 (95% CI 1.04-1.72; P=0.03). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Complete Renal Response and Safety
evidence.text: Complete renal response (UPCR <0.5, eGFR >=90% of preflare): 30% belimumab vs 20% placebo (OR 1.74). Time to renal-related event or death prolonged. Infections and serious adverse events balanced between arms.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by induction regimen, race, and country. D2 LOW - double-blind. D3 LOW - 86% completion. D4 LOW - central lab adjudication. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Clinical diagnosis of SLE by American College of Rheumatology (ACR) criteria.~* Biopsy confirmed active lupus nephritis.~* Clinically active lupus renal disease at screening requiring /receiving induction therapy with Standard of Care medications.~* Autoantibody-positive.~Key Exclusion Criteria:~* Pregnant or nursing.~* On dialysis within the past year.~* Treatment with belimumab within the past year .~* Receipt of induction therapy with cyclophosphamide within 3 month...

title: RapidMeta Rheumatology | Belimumab in SLE (BLISS Programme) v1.1
